steps.title: Spot the opening em dash and the parenthetical phrase
steps.content: The em dash after “Interpol—” opens a parenthetical phrase: “the International Criminal Police Organization,” which explains what Interpol is.

steps.title: Apply the matching em-dash rule
steps.content: An em dash opening a parenthetical phrase must be closed with a matching em dash. After “Organization,” an em dash properly closes the parenthetical phrase, creating “Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization—in 1971.” Using a comma, nothing, or a colon fails to close a parenthetical em-dash phrase. That matches choice B — B is correct.
